top_categories.workout_context: 184 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Milano-Sanremo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
Gran Fondo Milano-Sanremo's paved — Italian state and provincial roads, forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 184 miles.
At Gran Fondo Milano-Sanremo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
14,474ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
79ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
14,474ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 14,474ft of climbing on paved — Italian state and provincial roads, does to your power file on race day.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Milano-Sanremo. Train it until it feels boring.
184 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 184 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved — Italian state and provincial roads,. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 184 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 184 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo Milano-Sanremo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved — italian state and provincial roads, coastal roads, mountain pass to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 184 miles of paved — italian state and provincial roads, coastal roads, mountain pass in Milan to Sanremo, Liguria, Italy.
